attempt: Transfer TICKET46's rule: a finite symbolic grammar that only works by adding a horizon/depth escape coordinate cannot be promoted to a proof. It must either become a compact invariant theorem or produce a genuine counterexample.

bounded_result.discarded_shortcut: finite zero grammar repaired by a height-dependent escape coordinate
bounded_result.retained_target: A compact zero-clause grammar or positive kernel whose margin is invariant under all height extensions, not reselected from the checked height.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: an off-critical zero or kernel-pressure cycle that survives every compact repair

obstruction: The project has improved the falsification protocol for proposed proof grammars, but this transfer is not an infinite proof and not a certified counterexample.
candidate_theorem: A compact zero-clause grammar or positive kernel whose margin is invariant under all height extensions, not reselected from the checked height.
next_experiment: Audit whether zero-feature repairs depend on height; extract the first off-critical pressure cycle.
claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.
